Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. ***

Reach for the discount on the table: $200 * 15% = $30. So the discounted price is $200 - $30 = $170. For the chairs, $150 * 15% = $22.50. The discounted price is $150 - $22.50 = $127.50. The total amount the customer pays for both items is $170 + $127.50 = $297.50. The customer pays $297.50 for the items during the promotional week. ***

The promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. Sarah's shop supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products, and she is happy to offer a 15% discount on all items. The customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150. The customer pays $170 for the table and $127.50 for the chairs, totaling $297.50. The customer pays $297.50 for the items during the promotional week. ***
